mnemonics.title: Heavy Metals Remembered
mnemonics.content: LMA CAr (Lead, Mercury, Arsenic, Cadmium, Chromium) - LLead MAkes A CAr (Lead causes neurological damage, Mercury causes Minamata, Arsenic causes cancer, Cadmium causes Itai-itai disease, Chromium causes respiratory issues)

mnemonics.title: E-Waste Exposure Routes
mnemonics.content: IID - Inhalation, Ingestion, Dermal - 'I Eat Dirt' (remember children eating contaminated soil/dust)

mnemonics.title: PBT Chemicals in E-Waste
mnemonics.content: PBT = Persistent, Bioaccumulative, Toxic - 'People Breathe Toxins' remembering PBDEs, PCBs, Dioxins

mnemonics.title: Vulnerable Populations
mnemonics.content: CEP - Children, Elderly, Pregnant women - 'Carefully Expectant People need protection'

acronyms.usage: Brominated flame retardants in electronics, cause endocrine disruption
Toxic industrial chemicals, persistent organic pollutants causing cancer
Chemicals that persist in environment, bioaccumulate, and are toxic (e.g., dioxins, PCBs, PBDEs)
Older monitors/TVs containing lead and mercury
Manages Basel Convention regulating e-waste transboundary movement
Policy approach where manufacturers take responsibility for end-of-life management of their products

visualTips.visualization: Draw a food chain pyramid: algae (low toxin) → small fish (medium toxin) → large fish (high toxin) → birds/humans (highest toxin). Bioaccumulation = toxin buildup in ONE organism over time; Biomagnification = toxin INCREASES at each level going UP the pyramid.

Create a body diagram: Brain (Lead - 'Lead Head' for memory loss), Kidneys (Cadmium - 'Kidney-c'), Lungs (Chromium - 'Breathing problems'), Nerves (Mercury - 'Shaky Mercury'). Connect each metal to its target organ.
Draw a flow diagram: Landfill → Leachate (groundwater) → Soil → Plants → Animals → Humans. Show toxins moving through each step and accumulating.
Inverted pyramid: Prevention (top, most preferred) → Reuse → Recycle → Energy Recovery → Disposal (bottom, least preferred).

analogies.analogy: Like a bathtub with the drain partially open - water (toxins) comes in faster than it goes out, so the tub fills up (toxins accumulate in the body).
Like a chain of people passing a bucket of water - each person adds more water, so the last person gets the most water (most toxins).
These chemicals are like uninvited guests who never leave the party - they stay in the environment for decades and keep causing problems.
It's like exporting your trash to your neighbor's backyard - rich countries 'gift' their e-waste problems to developing nations who lack proper disposal infrastructure.
It's like picking apart a bomb to get the copper wire - dangerous work that puts immediate income over long-term health.
